newsSince: Within the window, nLIGHT showcased its production-stage 70kW HADES high-energy-laser weapon module at Eurosatory 2026 (Paris, June 15-19) and expanded Italian operations, reinforcing its directed-energy/defense pivot. This is positioning/marketing, not a new contract. Q1 2026 (revenue $80.2m, A&D revenue +69%) and the strategic pivot away from low-margin industrial cutting predate the window.

sources.label: Optics.org: nLIGHT record sales, laser-weapon demand

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/advanced-packaging alpha lens: nLIGHT's narrative is increasingly directed-energy/defense (Eurosatory HADES) rather than CPO/silicon-photonics/optical-DSP supply chain. The defense pivot is good for the company but dilutes the photonics-for-AI-connectivity thesis this page screens for. Hold rank 8 with a modest negative on lens fit.
verdicts.bull: Production-stage 70kW HADES, A&D revenue +69%, and Golden Dome/HELSI-2 exposure give a strong multi-year defense-laser growth runway; photonics IP underpins both defense and datacom optics.
verdicts.neutral: Strong company, but its momentum is defense lasers; CPO/AI-optics relevance is secondary and indirect.
verdicts.bear: After +66% YTD the easy re-rating is done; the directed-energy story is further from the CPO/HBM/package bottleneck this screen prizes, raising lens-mismatch and single-program concentration risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named directed-energy production contract (HADES/Golden Dome) or a datacom/CPO laser design win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Defense program timing/lumpiness and valuation after a 66% YTD run; CPO exposure thinner than rank implies.
